name: Back Door - Copper Cup
description: A plain wooden door at the rear of the Copper Cup, used by staff and late-night regulars. Crates and empty barrels stack nearby, and the smell of stew drifts out when it opens. A faint dark streak runs along one stone, as if something was once dragged past in a hurry.

name: Bar Counter
description: Shelves of bottles, a till, and crates tucked beneath a polished counter scarred by years of tankards. Mara keeps this vantage point to monitor every table and every rumor that enters the tavern.

name: Copper Cup
description: A low-beamed harbor tavern. Lanterns swing above crowded tables, and the air smells of stew, sea-salt, and woodsmoke. The barkeep Mara watches everything from behind the counter.

name: Docks
description: Wooden piers stretch out over dark water, crowded with fishing boats and the occasional merchant ship. Ropes creak, gulls wheel overhead, and the smell of tar and brine hangs thick in the air.

name: East Alley
description: A narrow alley where scrubbed cobbles never quite lose their stains. Laundry lines sway overhead, shutters crack open when footsteps pass, and several doorframes show marks that look recently cleaned.

name: Fishermen's Shacks
description: A cluster of small wooden shacks perched on pilings above the river. Nets hang to dry, and lantern-light spills from half-open doors late into the night. In more than one doorway, dark stains mar the floorboards with no memory of how they appeared.

name: Harbor Gate
description: An archway of weathered stone opening toward the piers. Guard posts on either side watch the flow of carts and sailors between the town and the docks.

name: Lumberyard
description: Stacks of freshly cut logs and the tang of sap fill the air. A small shack nearby shows scuffed floorboards and a corner where someone scrubbed at dried, dark stains.

name: Market Stalls
description: Canvas-topped stalls ring the edge of the square, half-closed for the evening. Fishmongers rinse boards, spice vendors shutter crates, and everyone trades the same uneasy gossip before heading home.

name: Old Shrine
description: A crumbling wayside shrine tucked into a bend of the alley, its carvings worn by sea-wind and salt. Offerings of shells and coins gather in a shallow stone bowl.

name: Old Well
description: An old stone well at the edge of the square, its rope replaced more than once. Locals say its water never runs dry, and that it connects to forgotten tunnels beneath the town. Faint rust-brown stains cling to some of the stones as though someone once washed something away here.

name: Riverside Path
description: A muddy path following the riverbank toward the outskirts. Reeds whisper in the breeze, and shallow boats are pulled up onto the shore.

name: Smuggler's Entrance
description: A half-concealed grate and narrow stone steps leading below the warehouses. The smell of damp and old river-mud clings to the air here.

name: South Bridge
description: A low stone bridge arching over a narrow river that feeds the harbor. Lanterns line the parapet, and you can see both the town walls and the distant open sea from its center. More than one passerby has reported waking here with aching feet and no memory of how they arrived.

name: Stair Landing
description: A narrow staircase creaks toward the upper rooms. A rope discourages casual wanderers.

name: Storeroom Door
description: A stout oak door with a brass four-dial combination lock. The correct code is 0415.

name: Temple of the Tide
description: A modest stone temple overlooking the square, its doors carved with waves and storm-clouds. Inside, candles flicker before a statue of a calm, watchful sea goddess. Many townsfolk come here seeking answers for the nights they cannot remember.

name: Town Hall
description: A timber-framed civic hall with a cluttered notice board and nervous clerks inside.

name: Town Square
description: The cobbled heart of the harbor town, lit by lanterns and restless conversation. Traders, guards, and dockhands all pass through here, and tonight nearly every conversation circles back to bloodstains, lost hours, and blame.

name: Warehouse Row
description: A line of squat stone warehouses hugs the waterfront. Their heavy doors and barred windows suggest both valuable cargo and things better kept out of sight. One doorframe bears a faded, rusty smear that no one can quite explain.

name: Watch Barracks
description: A sturdy building flying the town's colors. Racks of spears stand by the door, and off-duty guards swap stories on the steps when the weather is mild.

name: Wizard's House
description: A tall stone house with shuttered windows and a heavy iron fence, set just off the square. Lanterns burn late behind the curtains, and a pair of hired guards discourage casual callers.
